stemHtml:
Refer to figure.
An aircraft is overflying a mountainous area when the pilot observes the clouds shown in the attached figure. Mountain waves are frequently associated with this area. Therefore, a pilot flying in areas where these types of clouds are observed should consider the…

options.a: Impact on the mass and balance of the aeroplane since the upward and downward components of the airflow may cause significant pitching of the aeroplane.
options.b: Likelihood of significant clear air icing as a result of the air being forced over the mountains, creating an effect where ice may form outside clouds.
options.c: Performance of the aeroplane as the downward component at the back of the waves may be greater than the achievable rate of climb.
options.d: Risks of entering the area where the waves exist because the rate of climb may exceed what the aeroplane is capable of controlling.
